TipLfoRate: LFO speed. Slow values create gentle movement; high values add vibrato, chatter, and FX.
TipLfoWaveform: LFO shape. Sets whether modulation is smooth, stepped, noisy, or sharply switching.
TipOsc1Footage: OSC I range. Smaller foot values sound higher; 8' is the played note's base pitch.
TipOsc2Footage: OSC II range. Use a different range for octave layers, sub weight, or a second register.
TipOsc1Waveform: OSC I waveform. Sets the base tone: soft, bright, square, or pulse-like.
TipOsc2Waveform: OSC II waveform. Blend it with OSC I for thicker or more animated timbres.
TipOsc1LfoDepth: LFO depth for OSC I. Adds vibrato and pitch instability to the first oscillator.
TipOsc1FmDepth: FM from OSC II into OSC I. Low values animate the tone; high values become metallic and dirty.
TipOsc2Detune: OSC II detune against OSC I. Move slightly from center for width and beating.
TipOsc2LfoDepth: LFO depth for OSC II. Makes the second oscillator move and drift over time.
TipMixOsc1: OSC I mixer level. This is the main tonal layer.
TipMixOsc2: OSC II mixer level. Adds thickness, octave weight, beating, or a second color.
TipMixNoise: Noise mixer level. Useful for percussive attacks, wind, hiss, and rough texture.
TipFilterMode: Filter mode. Low-pass darkens the sound; band-pass leaves a narrow expressive band.
TipFilterEnvelopeLoop: Loops the filter envelope. Good for rhythmic wah and pulsing movement without a sequencer.
TipFilterCutoff: Filter cutoff, the main timbre control. Left is darker; right is brighter and more open.
TipFilterResonance: Resonance emphasizes the cutoff area. Adds whistle, acid bite, and aggression.
TipFilterEnvDepth: Envelope amount into the filter. Higher values make the attack open or shape the tone more strongly.
TipFilterLfoDepth: LFO amount into the filter. Higher values create stronger wah, auto-pulse, and cutoff motion.
TipFilterAttack: Filter envelope attack. Short is immediate; longer values fade brightness in.
TipFilterDecay: Filter envelope decay. Controls how quickly the filter falls back after the attack.
TipFilterSustain: Filter envelope sustain while a key is held. Higher values keep the sound brighter.
TipAmpAttack: Amplitude attack. Short is punchy; longer values fade the sound in.
TipAmpDecay: Amplitude decay from attack to sustain. Shapes the pluck and early body of the note.
TipAmpSustain: Held-note level. Lower values fade away; higher values keep the note steady.
TipAmpRelease: Tail after key release. Left is dry; right gives a longer fade-out.
TipAmpLfoDepth: LFO depth into amplitude. Adds tremolo and volume pulsing.
TipAmpEnvelopeLoop: Loops the amplifier envelope. Creates repeating volume pulses.
TipGateOn: Constant gate. Envelopes run without holding a key, useful for drones and FX.
TipMasterTune: Global synth tuning. Small offsets help match the instrument to a track.
TipMasterVolume: Main output volume. Reduce it if filter resonance or mixing overloads the signal.
TipHeadphoneVolume: Headphone monitor level. It changes listening level, not the synthesis character.
TipPortamento: Portamento. Higher values make notes glide into each other for longer.
TipDuophonic: Duophonic mode: OSC I follows the high note, OSC II follows the low note.
TipMainOutputEnabled: Enables or mutes the main synth output without resetting the current patch.
TipPianoKey: Key {0} (MIDI {1}). Pressing it plays a note through the current patch and envelopes.
FilterMode1: BP — keeps a band around cutoff
TipTestOutput: Plays a short 440 Hz sine through the selected device and format.
TipWaveGraph0: The LFO graph shows the shape and speed of low-frequency modulation for pitch, filter, and volume movement.
TipWaveGraph1: The OSC I graph shows the selected waveform of the first oscillator, the main tone source.
TipWaveGraph2: The OSC II graph shows the second oscillator, used for thickness, octaves, beating, or FM.
TipWaveGraph3: The mixer graph shows relative OSC I, OSC II, and noise levels before the filter.
TipWaveGraph4: The filter graph shows the approximate cutoff curve and resonance peak.
TipWaveGraph5: The amplifier graph shows the volume envelope: attack, decay, sustain, and release.
TipWaveGraph6: The output graph shows final signal motion after master volume and output control.
TipSection0: The modulator creates slow control motion for vibrato, wah, tremolo, and random effects.
TipSection1: Generator I is the main sound source. It controls range, waveform, and modulation depth.
TipSection2: Generator II adds a second tone for detune, octave layers, duophony, and FM.
TipSection3: The mixer blends OSC I, OSC II, and noise before the filter. Balance strongly changes density.
TipSection4: The filter shapes timbre by darkening, emphasizing cutoff, and moving from envelope or LFO.
TipSection5: The amplifier shapes note volume and tremolo, from clicks to pads and drones.
TipSection6: Master controls global tuning, volume, portamento, duophony, and the main output.
TipSignalFlow: Signal flow: modulation drives oscillators, then mixer, filter, amplifier, and master output.
TipKeyboard: The keyboard plays notes through the current patch. White and black keys work like a normal 3-octave keyboard.
